title: Block prompt injection instead of warn-only
source: OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications 2025 — LLM01: Prompt Injection + codebase audit of security_utils.py
description: Currently detect_injection_patterns() in security_utils.py only logs warnings when injection patterns are found but still passes the content to Claude subprocesses. OWASP LLM01 (2025) recommends defense-in-depth with blocking capability. The system should support a configurable enforcement mode that can reject or quarantine prompts with high-confidence injection patterns instead of just warning.
proposed_changes: 1. Add an `enforce_injection_block` mode to security_utils.py controlled by env var CTO_BLOCK_INJECTIONS (default: 'warn'). When set to 'block', detect_injection_patterns() raises a SecurityViolationError instead of just warning. 2. Add severity scoring to each injection pattern (high/medium/low). High-confidence patterns (e.g., 'ignore all previous instructions') block by default even in warn mode. 3. In delegate.py, orchestrate.py, and meeseeks.py, wrap the subprocess call path with a try/except for SecurityViolationError that logs the event via audit_log_security_event() and returns a safe error message instead of executing. 4. Add a quarantine log at .cto/logs/quarantined_prompts.jsonl for forensic review.
